aliases: US Navy Bureau of Medicine and Surgery
U.S. Navy Bureau of Medicine and Surgery
Navy Bureau of Medicine and Surgery
description: agency of the United States Department of the Navy
description_wiki: The Bureau of Medicine and Surgery (BUMED) is an agency of the United States Department of the Navy that manages health care activities for the United States Navy and the United States Marine Corps.

description: state of the United States of America
description_wiki: Pennsylvania, officially the Commonwealth of Pennsylvania, is a U.S. state located in the Mid-Atlantic, Northeastern, Appalachian, and Great Lakes regions of the United States.
Coraopolis () is a borough in Allegheny County, Pennsylvania, United States, along the Ohio River.

description: city and county seat in Allegheny County, Pennsylvania, United States
description_wiki: Pittsburgh ( PITS-burg) is a city in Allegheny County, Pennsylvania, United States, and its county seat.

description: valley and cultural region of western Virginia and the Eastern Panhandle of West Virginia in the United States
description_wiki: The Shenandoah Valley () is a geographic valley and cultural region of western Virginia and the eastern panhandle of West Virginia in the United States.

description: school building in Augusta County, Virginia
description_wiki: The Augusta Military Academy (AMA) was a secondary education military academy in Fort Defiance, Virginia, United States.

description: state-supported military college in Lexington, Virginia, USA
description_wiki: The Virginia Military Institute (VMI) is a public senior military college in Lexington, Virginia, United States.
The University of Virginia (UVA or Virginia) is a public research university located in Charlottesville City and Albemarle County in Virginia, United States.

surface_forms: hospital down at the end of Broad and Pattison Avenue
description: former hospital in Pennsylvania, United States
description_wiki: The Philadelphia Naval Hospital was the first high-rise hospital building constructed by the United States Navy.

description: U.S. Navy federal service academy in the Annapolis, Maryland area
description_wiki: The United States Naval Academy (USNA, Navy, or Annapolis) is a federal service academy adjacent to Annapolis, Maryland.

description: city in and county seat of Newport County, Rhode Island, United States
description_wiki: Newport is a seaside city on Aquidneck Island in Rhode Island, United States.

description: United States federal government public work relief program part of the New Deal
description_wiki: The Civilian Conservation Corps (CCC) was a government work relief program that ran from 1933 to 1942 in the United States for unemployed, unmarried men ages 18–25 and eventually expanded to ages 17–28, who volunteered amid widespread unemployment.

description: fair held at Flushing Meadows–Corona Park in Queens, New York
description_wiki: The 1939 New York World's Fair (also known as the 1939–1940 New York World's Fair) was an international exposition held at Flushing Meadows–Corona Park in Queens, New York City, United States.

surface_forms: Flushing Meadows, right around the lake from the pool
description: large public park in Queens, New York
description_wiki: Flushing Meadows–Corona Park (often referred to as Flushing Meadows Park or simply Flushing Meadows or Corona Park) is a public park in the northern part of Queens in New York City, New York, U.S. It is bounded by I-678 (Van Wyck Expressway) on the east, Grand Central Parkway on the west, Flushing Bay on the north, and Union Turnpike on the south.

description: maritime land force service branch of the United States Armed Forces
description_wiki: The United States Marine Corps (USMC), also referred to as the United States Marines or simply the Marines, is the naval infantry service branch of the United States Armed Forces.

aliases: Ground forces of the United States Armed Forces
description: land service branch of the United States Armed Forces
description_wiki: The United States Army (U.S. Army) is the land service branch of the United States Armed Forces.
